data.title: [Bug] Octahedron face photos appear brightened — textures missing sRGB colorSpace

data.to:
User-reported follow-up to FRAC-20: even after removing the per-face tint, photos still appear brightened/shifted vs the raw JPEG colors.

## Root cause
src/components/three/OctahedronHero.tsx loads textures via THREE.TextureLoader().loadAsync(path) but never sets tex.colorSpace. In Three.js r152+, color textures must be tagged with THREE.SRGBColorSpace — otherwise the raw JPEG data is treated as linear, and the renderer's linear→sRGB output transform brightens and washes them out.

## Fix
After each successful load, before storing the texture in state:
`tex.colorSpace = THREE.SRGBColorSpace;`

## Acceptance
- All 8 octahedron face photos render at colors visually matching their source JPEGs
- Build + tests baseline preserved

data.body: Impl + PR + merge complete. Single-line addition to usePerFaceMaterials in OctahedronHero.tsx (tex.colorSpace = THREE.SRGBColorSpace). Build passes; tests match master baseline (same 4 pre-existing failures). PR #155 merged as d13c754. Left useScrollingTextTexture CanvasTexture untagged — no user-reported issue with edge text colors.
